metadata.title: Classical Ethiopic (Geʽez) Lexicon
metadata.description: Authenticated lexicon based on canonical scholarly sources - Comprehensive Corpus Edition
sources: Dillmann, August. Lexicon linguae Aethiopicae. Leipzig: T.O. Weigel, 1865
Leslau, Wolf. Comparative Dictionary of Geʽez. Wiesbaden: Harrassowitz Verlag, 1987
Lambdin, Thomas O. Introduction to Classical Ethiopic (Geʽez). Scholars Press, 1978

linguistic_principles.attestation: Only attested forms from classical sources

metadata.coverage: Core vocabulary, high-frequency terms, and authentic corpus expansion - Batch 1

morphology.etymology: From Semitic root m-l-k meaning 'to rule, reign'

entries.notes: Royal title used in Ethiopian imperial tradition
Primary term for God in Classical Ethiopic religious texts

usage_examples.geez: በስመ አብ ወወልድ ወመንፈስ ቅዱስ አሀዱ አምላክ
usage_examples.transliteration: ba-sǝma ʾab wa-wald wa-manfas qǝddus ʾaḥadu ʾamlāk
usage_examples.translation: In the name of the Father and Son and Holy Spirit, one God
Our Father who art in heaven

entries.notes: Symbol of imperial power in Ethiopian tradition
Royal symbol, Lion of Judah tradition
Biblical patriarch, ancestor of Ethiopian royal house
Classical name for the Ethiopian highlands

usage_examples.translation: In the name of the Father and Son and Holy Spirit

notes.historical: Title used for Ethiopian emperors, notably the Negus of Aksum
Famous example: Queen of Sheba (ንግሥተ ሳባ)

entries.etymology: From root f-q-r relating to caring/cherishing
From Semitic root ʾ-m-n (to be firm, reliable)

morphology.etymology: From Semitic root b-r-h meaning 'to shine, be bright'
From Semitic root s-b-h meaning 'to praise'
From Semitic root n-g-r meaning 'to beat, strike'

entries.earliest_attestation: Chronicle of Zar'a Ya'eqob, 15th century CE

From Arabic ʿālam via Islamic influence

entries.earliest_attestation: Book of Enoch translation, 6th century CE

morphology.etymology: From Hebrew Mōšeh via Greek Mōusês
